items.title: Learn T-SQL With Erik: Aligning Queries and Indexes Part 2
items.author: Erik Darling (Erik Darling Data)
items.summary:
In this video, I delve into the nuances of query and index alignment in SQL Server, specifically focusing on how to optimize queries using indexes more effectively. We explore a scenario where we have multiple indexes on related tables but need to rewrite our queries to better leverage these indexes. By experimenting with different query hints and techniques, such as using temporary tables and outer applies, I demonstrate practical methods to improve performance. The goal is not just to show you how to fix the issue at hand, but also to provide insights that can be applied to similar situations in your own database environments. Whether you're a seasoned SQL Server professional or just starting out, there's always something new to learn from these detailed walkthroughs.

CHAPTERS

00:00:00 - Introduction
00:02:45 - Problematic Query Analysis
00:06:38 - Index and Predicate Issues
00:09:21 - Optimizing the Query with Outer Apply
00:11:24 - Improved Performance Explanation
00:12:55 - Conclusion

items.title: Don’t Let Your Data Monster Destroy Your Momentum
items.author: Confluent
items.summary:
Every enterprise has a data monster. And a way to take control. From AI to analytics, Confluent supercharges innovation across every organization with reusable, reliable, real-time data. 

So don’t let your data monster hold you back. Unlock value and unleash business impact with freeflowing, real-time data on The World’s Data Streaming Platform.

ABOUT CONFLUENT
Confluent is pioneering a fundamentally new category of data infrastructure focused on data in motion. Confluent’s cloud-native offering is the foundational platform for data in motion – designed to be the intelligent connective tissue enabling real-time data, from multiple sources, to constantly stream across the organization. With Confluent, organizations can meet the new business imperative of delivering rich, digital front-end customer experiences and transitioning to sophisticated, real-time, software-driven backend operations. To learn more, please visit www.confluent.io

items.title: Ep. 3 - Durable execution, reliability engineering, and the future of agentic AI with Jeremy Edberg
items.author: Redpanda Data
items.summary:
Durable execution has been solving reliability problems for decades. Now it's the missing primitive for agentic AI systems.

We're joined by Jeremy Edberg, C-Suite Advisor* at DBOS, Inc., to explore how durable execution works, why it matters for building production-ready AI agents, and what lessons from Netflix-scale reliability engineering apply directly to the age of agentic coding.

RESOURCES MENTIONED:
DBOS, Inc https://www.dbos.dev
Transact library https://www.dbos.dev/dbos-transact
CockroachDB https://www.cockroachlabs.com
Dosu https://dosu.dev/

Thanks for listening to “Hello Agent!: The podcast at the intersection of data & agents.” If you loved this episode, let us know with a 5-star review! Remember to subscribe so you don’t miss an episode. To learn more about Redpanda, visit redpanda.com/

items.title: Stop Waiting for Your Data: How to Take Control
items.author: Confluent Developer
items.summary:
https://confluent.io | Most organizations treat data ingestion as a passive activity, waiting for data to arrive on someone else's schedule, in someone else's format, with no control over quality or timing. The result? Data that exists but isn't accessible when and where it's needed.

Data ingestion is the bridge between where data is created and where it delivers value, but the way you build that bridge determines how much control you actually have. This video reveals the four critical dimensions of ingestion control: quality, format, timing, and access, and shows why the choice between batch and streaming ingestion determines whether you're driving your pipeline or waiting on it.

We'll follow the transformation of a hospital's data systems from paper records in filing cabinets to real-time patient data streaming to every workstation, and show you how to apply that same transformation to your own data systems. You'll explore traditional batch ingestion methods and their limitations, streaming alternatives like event-driven CDC and Kafka Connect, and when to skip ingestion entirely when your data is already flowing through a streaming platform. We'll also cover a key design principle: even if your sources are batch today, build your pipeline for streaming, so you're ready when they catch up.

Technologies covered: Apache Kafka, Kafka Connect, Debezium, Change Data Capture (CDC), Data Pipelines

📚 CHAPTERS:
00:00-00:35 Intro
00:35-01:30 Transformation to real-time data
01:30-03:04 Before Data Ingestion
03:04-03:48 After Data Ingestion
03:48-07:02 Ingestion Methods
07:02-07:56 Conclusion

LEARN MORE
► Confluent Developer: https://developer.confluent.io

items.title: From Modern Data Stack to the Coding Data Stack (CDS): Hopsworks 5.0
items.author: Hopsworks
items.summary:
In this webinar, we introduced the Coding Data Stack (CDS) in Hopsworks 5.0 and demonstrated how coding agents like Claude Code are changing the way teams build ML pipelines, real-time AI systems, dashboards, and data applications. These new capabilities will soon be available in Hopsworks.

We also walked through a live demo using a Football World Cup prediction use case, showing how coding agents inside a terminal can be used to build and run ML pipelines to predict the tournament winner.

Watch the demo to see how production-ready Data/AI pipelines can be built and deployed faster with a coding-agent-first workflow.
